FHOltOAY DANCES AXE NUM-


EROUs.
One of the many dances planned
Tor the holidays that will be an
event of note is the high school
alumni dance to be given Tuesday
evening, January 3, in the ball
room of the new Woman's club on
Ninth avenue and Walnut street
The committee on arrangements is
Clayton Evanson, Wayne Butter
baugh. Clair Daniels, Ralph White
and Clay Strong. Patronesses for
the event will be Miss Wright, Miss
S.egler, Miss Palmer and Miss
Kayne, all members of the high
school faculty.


Monday evening, December 26.
the Chanticler club, composed of
members and alumni of the high
school, will dance in Masonic tenn
pie, and Thursday evening, De.
cember 20, the Billikens, another
high school club, will hold a dane
ing party in the same hall. A fea-
ture OF this event will be the sing
ing of numbers by the Billiken
quartet.


WoMAN's BOOK CLUB HELD


CHRISTMAS PARTY.


The Christmas meeting of the
Woman's Book club was held yes
terday afternoon at the home of
Mrs. L. B. Gates, 622 Augusta ave-
nue.. An extensive and delightful
program was rendered by the club
members and gifts were brought
and a box made up for the Old
Ladies' home.


& 4 q


WILL HOLD FIRST MEETING


OF YEAR.


Members of the Mantle Boule-
ward club will hold their first meet
ing of the year on New Years ave
at the home of Mr.. and Mrs. Cliff
ton Darling. 1916 Mantle boulevard.
Cards will furnish the form of en-
tertainment. This is to be the first
meeting of series that will con-
tine throughout the season.

ELECT OFFICERS OF MASONIC


LODGE.


At the meeting of Spokane coun-
Gil, No. 4, Royal and Select Mas
tiers of the Ancient Masons, last
night in the Masonic temple, Ed.
ward j. VOss was elected thrice
illustrious grand master and James
w. Bowen, right illustrious master.
Judge Henry l.. KEENAN, was select
ed nv tho members &C troocuro,.
and Harry Rush as recorder.
